extra.reasoning: The safety property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. The only transition that can set sent:=true while opted_out is true is t_resume_glitch, guarded by (opted_out and acc==13). I decoded each step event: the nested if-then-else chain just performs modular reduction, so step_0 = (3*acc+2) mod 16, step_1 = (3*acc+10) mod 16, step_2 = (3*acc+6) mod 16 (verified the nested form equals mod 16 for all acc in [0,15], and that the intermediate max 55 needs at most 3 subtractions of 16). I simulated the recurrence from acc=0 via BFS and full closure: the reachable acc set is exactly {0,2,4,6,8,10,12,14}. acc=13 is unreachable. This is a parity invariant: acc starts even (0); 3*acc preserves parity, each constant added (2,10,6) is even, and the modulus 16 is even, so acc remains even on every reachable state forever. The target 13 is odd, hence the glitch guard never fires. All other send paths are safe: t_send_allowed requires not opted_out, t_send_blocked sets sent:=false, optout sets sent:=false, and every step sets sent:=false. Reachability is fully closed within 4 steps, well inside the 18-event bound. No event sequence drives acc to 13 after opt-out, so no counterexample exists.
